comments.text: A. R. Singh in [Si26] has proved that: $f(k^{2}+1)=k \text{ for all } k\in\mathbb{N}$, if and only if the sum $\sum_{k=1}^{\infty}(f(k^{2}+1)-k)$ converges. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

I've used Aristotle to generate a Lean formalization of Baek-Koizumi-Ueoro (2024)'s proof that $g(k^2+2c+1)=k+c/k$ for any $-k<c<k$, with a remark from Terence Tao that the proof works without the arguments of Praton.
